measurements.value: 1px solid (from spec - 32px margin-top, 32px padding-top above border)

root.description: Full-width block container with border-top separator. The border-top has 32px margin above and 32px padding below (from spec).

children.description: Max-width wrapper. Flex layout with items-center and justify-between. Heading on left, social links on right.
Display heading using Anton font at 24px. Figma shows 'Follow Us' but content is editable. Uses Headline/H7 design token style. Color is black.
Flex container (nav element for accessibility) holding repeatable link items. Gap of 16px between items.
Individual social media link. Flex row with icon (24x24) + text label. Gap 12px, vertical padding 8px. This is a repeatable inner block item.
Social media icon, 24x24px. SVG rendered inline for currentColor inheritance. Examples: Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube.
Text label next to the icon. Roboto Medium 12px, charcoal color (#1f1f1d). Examples: 'Facebook', 'Instagram', 'TikTok', 'Youtube'.

possiblyOptional.reason: The 'Follow Us' heading could be optional since some contexts may want only icons. However, the Figma design prominently features it as a core element.
possiblyOptional.visualEvidence: Heading takes up the left side of the layout in the Figma screenshot.

possiblyOptional.reason: Some social media blocks show only icons without text labels. This design shows labels next to every icon. Could be a toggle (show/hide labels).
possiblyOptional.visualEvidence: Every link item in the Figma design shows both icon and text label.

innerBlocks.name: WideSocialMediaItem
innerBlocks.reason: Each social link (icon + label + URL) is a repeatable unit. The design shows 4 items (Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube) but the block should allow adding/removing items. This follows the SocialMediaItem pattern from the social-media block type.

layoutRecommendations.approach: The main container uses flex with space-between to push heading left and social links right. The social links group is also a flex container with 16px gap. Each link item is a flex row with 12px gap between icon and label.

responsive.desktop: Heading on left, social links on right in a single horizontal row. The Figma design width is 850px which fits within the standard container.
responsive.mobile: Per spec: 'display items stacked vertically, aligned to the left'. The heading should stack above the social links. The social link items should also stack vertically (one below the other) and be left-aligned.

layoutRecommendations.borderTop: The spec explicitly requires a border-top with 32px margin-top and 32px padding-top. This creates a visual separator between this block and content above it. Use border-t with appropriate margin and padding utilities.
notes: This block is NOT a standard social media icon-only block. It has a wider layout with a heading on the left and icon+text links on the right.
The heading uses the H7 design token (Anton, 24px, regular weight). Important: Anton font appears slanted by design but is NOT italic.
Link labels use Roboto Medium 12px -- this is the Body/Body - Small design token.
Icons are 24x24 and should use inline SVG rendering for currentColor support.
The 475px heading width in Figma is a layout constraint, not a fixed width for implementation. Use flex-grow or similar to let content fill available space.
The social links container aligns to the right on desktop. On mobile, everything stacks left-aligned.
Each link item has 8px vertical padding, creating a 40px total height (24px icon + 2*8px padding).
The block is a two-level parent/child structure following the Social Media block type pattern: WideSocialMedia (parent with heading + nav) contains WideSocialMediaItem (child, repeatable link items).

keyDifferencesFromStandardSocialMedia: Standard social media block: icon-only, no text labels, smaller footprint, usually nested inside footer/header sections.
Wide social media block: full-width section with heading on left, icon+text links on right. Standalone block, not nested inside another section.
Standard social media uses <nav><ul><li> structure. This block wraps the same <nav> structure but adds a heading alongside it in a flex container.
Standard social media items have only logoId and link. This block's items additionally display a visible text label next to the icon.
